title: Migrate auth from python-jose to PyJWT
summary: Replace the deprecated python-jose JWT library with the maintained PyJWT across local HS256 auth and OIDC RS256 validation, keeping token behavior identical at the API boundary.
motivation: python-jose is unmaintained and emits deprecation warnings on every run; PyJWT is the maintained standard. Salvaged from closed PR #8 (feature/service-layer-refactor).

included: Replace `from jose import jwt, JWTError` usage in app/auth/auth.py, app/auth/dependencies.py, and app/auth/oidc_validator.py with PyJWT equivalents
Local HS256 access/refresh token encode and decode keep the same claims, expiry, and rejection behavior
OIDC RS256 JWKS validation keeps the same signature, issuer, and audience checks
Drop python-jose from backend/pyproject.toml, add PyJWT, and refresh uv.lock
excluded: Changing token claims, lifetimes, SECRET_KEY, or ALGORITHM configuration
Changing the OIDC opaque-token userinfo validation path (non-JWT)
Changing any auth endpoint, request/response schema, or the get_current_user contract

design_facts.stack: FastAPI/Python 3.13 backend. Hybrid auth: local JWT (HS256) plus OIDC via Authelia (RS256 JWKS for JWT access tokens, userinfo endpoint for opaque tokens).

work_items.title: Swap python-jose for PyJWT
work_items.summary: Move all JWT encode/decode in the auth modules to PyJWT while preserving local HS256 and OIDC RS256 behavior at the API boundary, then drop python-jose from the dependency set.

exposes.note: Auth function signatures stay the same (create_access_token, token decode, get_current_user); only the underlying JWT library changes.

behavior: Local HS256 tokens are issued and validated by PyJWT with the same claims and expiry
Expired, malformed, and wrong-signature tokens are rejected with the existing status codes
OIDC RS256 tokens are validated against JWKS with the same issuer and audience checks
python-jose is removed from pyproject.toml and uv.lock; PyJWT is added

oracle.command: make test-backend-lint && make test-backend-format && make test-backend-types && cd backend && uv run pytest tests/unit/auth/test_auth.py tests/unit/auth/test_dependencies.py tests/unit/auth/test_oidc_validator.py tests/unit/auth/test_oidc_error_handling.py tests/integration/auth/test_auth_router.py tests/integration/auth/test_oidc_hybrid_auth.py -v
assertions: Local login issues an HS256 token that get_current_user accepts, and tampered or expired tokens are rejected with the existing status codes.
OIDC RS256 JWT validation still passes issuer, audience, and signature checks against JWKS.
No `jose` import remains in app/auth; python-jose is gone from pyproject.toml and PyJWT is present.
